cards.name: Sprig Trestle
cards.note: The apprentice crowd-caller: seats onlookers to match your joinery count. Every run's first way of filling the room.

cards.name: Pegged Bench
cards.note: The apprentice chain-piece — adds a link so your next play lands harder. Teaches the habit every Way builds on: don't stall.

cards.name: Loose Thread
cards.note: The plainest of pieces: it just resists the Paling one extra season. No combo, no trick — a safe hold in the early hand.

cards.name: Kettle-Loaf
cards.note: Plain bread: a flat bit of delight, no combo, no setup. The dependable baseline your first mornings lean on.

cards.name: Hearth-Hum
cards.note: Sprig Trestle's song-side twin: seats onlookers to match your song count.

cards.name: Grey Bowl
cards.note: The glaze twin of the apprentice crowd-callers: onlookers to match your glaze count. Gleaners-to-be lean on it early.

cards.name: First Step
cards.note: The dance twin of the apprentice crowd-callers: onlookers to match your dance count. A Fairwright's first stage.

cards.name: Setterby Trestle
cards.note: Opens the Kilnfast chain: the crowd it seats grows with every piece you've fired for good, and it braces the chain while it builds.

cards.name: Calipers at the Bench
cards.note: The middle of the chain: pours spare attention onto your held capstone, and once woken it stamps off-suit cards 'joinery' so the engine counts them.

cards.name: The Fired Beam
cards.note: The Kilnfast payoff: wakes itself off your room, then fills askings by how much you've fired for good. By winter it's the biggest ordinary morning there is.

cards.name: Bank the Coals
cards.note: Steady fuel: a crowd off your joinery count plus a chain link. Keeps the room warm while the big pieces mature.

cards.name: Seasoned Timber
cards.note: Second line of the engine: crowd from your fired pieces, and once woken it stamps spare cards 'joinery' to feed the count.

cards.name: Keep the Kiln
cards.note: Insurance: braces the chain against a stall and holds itself fresh a season. The quiet card that stops a bad morning becoming a broken one.

cards.name: Dovetail Draw
cards.note: Consistency at dawn: digs a joinery piece from your pack each morning so the engine never starves.

cards.name: The Last Lighting
cards.note: Opens the Eveners' round: composts a dead card in hand into room now, and one in the pack into the town's table. Waste, recycled twice.

cards.name: Bright Hand to Hand
cards.note: Keeps the purse moving: carves handsels out of your spare room while the chain grows. Money that would have gone dull, made bright.

cards.name: Even the Rim
cards.note: The Eveners' payoff, restored: wakes itself on the room your retires fattened, fills by your chain, and hands a card back on each fulfilment.

cards.name: Waymeet Gift
cards.note: A gift on the road: composts a dead card into the town's table and keeps your chain moving. Thin your deck, thicken the town.

cards.name: The Turning Purse
cards.note: Money in motion: carves handsels from spare room and replaces itself. The purse never sits.

cards.name: Even Fuel
cards.note: The on-ramp: a dead card becomes room right now, plus a link. The cheapest way to start the compost round.

cards.name: Quick Feet
cards.note: Dawn consistency: a dance piece each morning keeps the round supplied.

cards.name: Paper-Bird
cards.note: The signature dart: a chain-sized fill and it replaces itself. One-shot — it flies once, but the flock is many.

cards.name: Quick-Hand
cards.note: Tempo glue: a card and a braced link. Keeps the thin deck spinning without risk.

cards.name: The Standing Count
cards.note: The Untold payoff: wakes off your chain, fills by it, and pays two cards on fulfilment. Pace, converted into tonnage.

cards.name: Thread the Needle
cards.note: Makes everything thread: stamps a spare card into the suit and digs for the next. The count must keep counting.

cards.name: Swift Tally
cards.note: A dart with a link in it: chain-sized fill, then keeps the chain moving. Cheap, fast, gone.

cards.name: Keep the Pace
cards.note: The rhythm-keeper: a braced link and a card each time the chain moves. Never stall, never starve.

cards.name: Morning Flock
cards.note: A thread piece every dawn — the flock replenishes itself.

cards.name: Raise the Wain-Stage
cards.note: Raises the venue: a flat six-seat crowd and a braced chain. The show starts with a stage.

cards.name: Pitch the Ring
cards.note: Doubles down: seats a crowd the size of the room you already have, and stamps a spare card 'dance'. The ring grows the ring.

cards.name: The Whole-Fair Turn
cards.note: The one enormous show: wakes off the whole room, fills by your dance troupe, and turns everything past its ceiling into gleam. The turn the run was for.

cards.name: Bunting and Banners
cards.note: Dressing the venue: a crowd matching your room, another hand stamped 'dance'. Spectacle stacked on spectacle.

cards.name: Call the Crowd
cards.note: A dependable barker: four seats and a brace. Fills the benches while the big turn assembles.

cards.name: String the Bunting
cards.note: Crowd off your woken troupe plus a link — the venue crew that scales with the show.

cards.name: The Ringmaster's Draw
cards.note: A dance piece each dawn: the ringmaster always has the next act ready.

cards.name: Keep the Silver Singing
cards.note: Opens the courtship: two braced links and your cheapest card stamped 'song'. The proper introductions, made early.

cards.name: Court the Singing-Silver
cards.note: The courtship itself: with a chain of three standing, wins the singing-silver over. Your gleam is checked, never spent.

cards.name: The Silver Refrain
cards.note: The wooed payoff: wakes off the room, fills by your chain, and returns a song card each fulfilment — the highest steady delight in the pool.

cards.name: Tune the Strings
cards.note: Warm-up scales: two braced links, a card into 'song'. Sets the term the courtship will ask for.

cards.name: The Standing Introduction
cards.note: The easy introduction: courts silver on the lightest term — a single link. How a Mannerly deck gets its first proud piece.

cards.name: Grace-Note
cards.note: A polite crowd off your song count and a link — small manners that keep the room listening.

cards.name: Silver Warm-Up
cards.note: A song piece each dawn: rehearsal never stops.

cards.name: Walk the Pale
cards.note: Walks into the fading on purpose: a crowd from the town's grey, handsels from the season's fade. The braver the route, the richer the morning.

cards.name: Glean the Ground
cards.note: Feeds the big piece from bad ground: pours room onto your held capstone while the grey seats a crowd.

cards.name: Ripe Mending
cards.note: The harvest and the healing: fills by your woken glaze, and every fulfilment mends this town's fading. The only Way that leaves places better.

cards.name: Grey-Gleaning
cards.note: Harvest crew: a grey-sized crowd and a spare card stamped 'glaze'. The count grows where others won't go.

cards.name: Mend the Verge
cards.note: Harvests the edge and mends it after: a grey-fed crowd, then healing on fulfilment.

cards.name: Slow the Grey
cards.note: The gentle start: a crowd off your glaze count and a link — before the deep grey pays, the shallow grey teaches.

cards.name: Pale Harvest
cards.note: A glaze piece each dawn: the harvest never lacks hands.

cards.name: Stoke the Long Fire
cards.note: Mid-run filler: turns your fired-for-good count directly into asking progress without waiting for the capstone.

cards.name: Even the Coalbed
cards.note: Fills by your chain length and braces it — a Kilnfast fill that rewards playing clean, not just growing tall.

cards.name: Trestle Run
cards.note: A cheap tempo piece: a small crowd off your fired count, a link for the chain. Early rhythm while the kiln heats.

cards.name: Set the Keel-Block
cards.note: The sturdier tempo piece: crowd off your fired count with a braced link, for the mornings that must not slip.

cards.name: Sort the Offcuts
cards.note: Digs for the joinery piece you need now, rather than at dawn. Utility for assembling the chain on time.

cards.name: Warm the Workshop
cards.note: Plain warmth, Kilnfast-stamped: flat delight, no combo. Filler that never wastes a slot entirely.

cards.name: Feed and Fire
cards.note: The engine-builder: pours your room onto the held capstone, and once woken it starts seating crowds off your fired count.

cards.name: The Ember Crown
cards.note: Too proud to buy — the kiln's crowning piece. Wakes off your room, fills by your fired count, and pays a card back on every fulfilment.

cards.name: The Dusk-Gift
cards.note: Compost into progress: burns a dead card for room, fills by your chain, and links on. The Eveners' whole plan on one card.

cards.name: Chainwise Gift
cards.note: A bigger dusk-gift without the link: dead card in, chain-sized fill out.

cards.name: Left for the Morning
cards.note: Tidies the pack overnight: a dead card becomes worth on the town's table, and the chain keeps its step.

cards.name: Season's Shavings
cards.note: Carves handsels that grow as the season fades, braced against the stall. Late-year money for the steady hand.

cards.name: Widening Round
cards.note: Card flow, twice: one now, one each dawn. The round widens on its own.

cards.name: Brace the Waychain
cards.note: The safety piece: a card, a link, and a brace so the next stall doesn't cool anything.

cards.name: Even by Lastlight
cards.note: The little engine: compost a dead card into room, then carve that room into handsels. Nothing wasted, in one motion.

cards.name: The Great Un-Hoarding
cards.note: The proud finale of thrift: composts a card, wakes itself off your chain, and fills by it. Everything saved, spent at once — the Eveners' one showy morning.

cards.name: Running Tally
cards.note: A dart that recruits: chain-sized fill, and your cheapest card joins the thread count.

cards.name: Marked in Passing
cards.note: Fill in passing, stamp in passing: a one-shot that grows the count as it scores.

cards.name: Dawn Errand
cards.note: Double dawn draw — the fastest hands in Brightwell start with the fullest hands.

cards.name: Nimble Step
cards.note: A burst of two cards and a link: the step that keeps the thin deck ahead of trouble.

cards.name: Chalk the Count
cards.note: Chalks your cheapest card into the thread count and digs onward. Bookkeeping at a run.

cards.name: Hold the Thread
cards.note: The only piece that works on a stall: it patches the chain the moment it would break. Insurance for a Way that lives on momentum.

cards.name: The Little Ledger
cards.note: A small self-waking engine: wakes off your chain and adds a flat fill. Modest, repeatable, very Untold.

cards.name: The Long Tally
cards.note: The proud sum of all the small: wakes off the chain, fills by it, and pays two cards per fulfilment. Every dart counted.

cards.name: Feed the Crown
cards.note: Progress between shows: fills by your troupe with the chain held safe. Not every morning can be the big one.

cards.name: Pour the Turnout
cards.note: The mid-size show: a troupe-sized fill and four more seats. Keeps standing up between spectacles.

cards.name: Ring Up the Lanterns
cards.note: Lights the ring: a crowd off the troupe, a link on the chain. Cheap venue prep.

cards.name: Throng the Ring
cards.note: Five seats, braced: the throng arrives early and stays through a stall.

cards.name: The Callboy's Round
cards.note: The callboy fetches the next act and holds the chain. Utility with stage manners.

cards.name: Spill the Ring Over
cards.note: The rehearsal for the big one: wakes off your room and spills its own overflow into gleam. Overkill, on purpose.

cards.name: Tally the Ring
cards.note: Counts the house and stamps another act 'dance'. The room, made bigger by measuring it.

cards.name: Crown the Round-Fair
cards.note: The proud finale: seats a room-sized crowd, wakes off all of it, and brims the excess into gleam. When it lands, the run is won.

cards.name: The Refrain Repeated
cards.note: Fills by your woken songs and stamps the next voice in. The refrain grows by repetition.

cards.name: Answer in Kind
cards.note: A courteous reply: a chain-sized fill and a link onward. Progress with manners.

cards.name: Rehearse the Courtesies
cards.note: The cheap rehearsal: braced links and a stamped card, for setting terms on a budget.

cards.name: A Word of Introduction
cards.note: A middle introduction: courts silver on a two-link term. For when the easy jar is spent and the grand term isn't ready.

cards.name: Listen for the Answer
cards.note: Two song cards at once: listening is how the next courtship is found.

cards.name: Note What the Room Loves
cards.note: The cheapest manners: stamp a spare card 'song' and seat the crowd that answers. A tiny card that grows the whole court.

cards.name: Hold the Note
cards.note: The patient engine: wakes off your chain, then fills by your woken songs each morning after.

cards.name: The Silver Wedding
cards.note: The courtship's grand prize: wakes off the whole room and fills by every woken song. Set it up right and it lands like a wedding.

cards.name: Windrow Mending
cards.note: Fills by your woken glaze and stamps another hand for the harvest. Work and recruitment in one pass.

cards.name: Attend the Ripe
cards.note: Attends the ripest ground: a glaze-fed fill plus a grey-fed crowd. The deeper the fade, the better it works.

cards.name: Read the Gatepost
cards.note: Reads the town before working it: a small grey-fed crowd and a card stamped 'glaze'. Cheap scouting for the harvest.

cards.name: Comb the Verge
cards.note: Combs what the season dropped: a crowd that grows as the year fades, plus a link.

cards.name: Sift the Sheaves
cards.note: Digs for the glaze piece the harvest needs now.

cards.name: Ease the Grey
cards.note: Recruitment that heals: stamps a hand 'glaze' and mends the town on each fulfilment.

cards.name: Spiral-Reader's Round
cards.note: The full round: a grey-fed crowd, a stamped hand, a link. The reader's whole craft on one card.

cards.name: The Ripe Hour
cards.note: The proudest harvest: fills by every woken glaze, wakes off the room, and mends the land with each fulfilment. The grey, repaid.
